title: How Can You Improve Project Portfolio Management in monday.com?

posts.author: Teresa.DevSamurai (Teresa Tran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are you struggling to manage multiple projects effectively in monday.com? While monday.com is great for individual projects, it often lacks the tools needed for managing a whole portfolio. This can lead to issues like poor resource planning, limited visibility, and difficulty tracking progress across all your projects.

In this blog, we’ll look at how you can enhance your project portfolio management (PPM) in monday.com. We’ll discuss some of its limitations and introduce the PPM app, which can help you overcome these challenges and make managing your projects much easier.

Project Portfolio Management in monday.com1020×574 53.9 KB

What is Project Portfolio Management (PPM)?

Project Portfolio Management (PPM) is more than just overseeing individual projects—it’s about managing all projects in a way that aligns with your organization’s goals and resources. This approach helps ensure that every project contributes to your business’s overall success.

Multiple Project Management1020×574 49.8 KB

Key Components of PPM:

  1. Project Selection and Prioritization: Choosing the right projects to work on and ranking them based on their importance and impact.
  2. Resource Management: Allocating the right people, time, and budget to projects to maximize efficiency and avoid overloading your team.
  3. Risk Management: Identifying potential issues that could impact projects and finding ways to mitigate them.
  4. Performance Tracking: Monitoring progress and performance across all projects to ensure they stay on track and deliver expected outcomes.

While monday.com offers some tools for these tasks, it’s often not enough for comprehensive PPM. In the next section, we’ll explore the limitations of monday.com and how you can address them.

PPM in monday.com: Features and Limitations

monday.com offers several useful features for managing projects, but when it comes to Project Portfolio Management (PPM), it has some constraints that may limit its effectiveness for complex project environments. Here’s a closer look at what monday.com offers and where it falls short.

Key PPM Features in monday.com:

1. Project Inventory and Overview:

  • Create a portfolio management board to get a high-level view of all projects in one place.
  • Easily link portfolio-level boards to more detailed project boards managed by individual teams.

2. Customizable Columns and Fields:

  • Use custom columns to track key metrics such as funding status, resources allocated, and project priorities.
  • Quickly evaluate projects based on factors like estimated value, last evaluation, and overall project score.

Customizable Columns and Fields1020×574 43.1 KB

3. Dashboards for Reporting and Analysis:

  • Build custom dashboards that provide real-time snapshots of project performance, profitability, and health.
  • Integrate data from multiple boards to get a holistic view of your portfolio.

Dashboards for Reporting and Analysis1020×574 39.5 KB

4. Task Ownership and Accountability:

  • Assign specific tasks to team members, ensuring clear accountability and streamlined communication.
  • Monitor task progress and address potential risks before they escalate.

Limitations of monday.com in PPM:

1. Limited Portfolio-Level Insights:

  • While individual project management is strong, monday.com lacks built-in features for comprehensive portfolio-level management, making it difficult to track performance across all projects.

2. Resource Management Gaps:

  • Basic resource tracking tools are available, but they do not provide a detailed view of resource allocation across multiple projects, making it challenging to manage team capacity and prevent overload.

3. Complex Project Dependencies:

  • Managing dependencies between projects is cumbersome, which can lead to scheduling conflicts and delays in complex portfolios.

4. Risk Management and Strategic Alignment:

  • monday.com offers basic risk tracking, but it lacks advanced tools for proactively managing risks and aligning projects with strategic goals.

These limitations can hinder effective project portfolio management, especially for organizations handling multiple, interdependent projects. In the next section, we’ll explore how these gaps can be addressed and how you can enhance your PPM capabilities in monday.com.

Enhancing PPM in monday.com

While monday.com offers essential project management tools, adding advanced features can significantly enhance your Project Portfolio Management (PPM) capabilities. The “Project & Portfolio Management with Advanced Gantt Chart” solution integrates seamlessly with monday.com, providing a more robust platform to manage complex projects and portfolios. Let’s dive into how this solution can elevate your PPM experience.

=>> Try PPM app for monday.com now

Key Features for Enhanced PPM

1. Project Portfolio Management:

  • Unlimited Project Connections: Manage an unlimited number of projects within your portfolio, providing a comprehensive overview of all your initiatives in one place, without any restrictions on connected projects.
  • Holistic Portfolio View: Get a big-picture perspective of your entire portfolio, allowing you to monitor performance, resources, and strategic alignment across all projects seamlessly.

Project Portfolio Management1020×574 26.3 KB

2. Gantt Chart Integration:

  • Visualize Timelines and Dependencies: The Gantt chart view allows you to easily see project timelines, dependencies, and how tasks interconnect. This visual representation helps in understanding project flow and identifying potential bottlenecks.
  • Dependency & Auto Scheduling: Automatically adjust task start dates based on dependencies, ensuring that any changes in one task’s schedule are reflected across all related tasks, optimizing project efficiency.
  • Baselines: Set baselines to compare your planned schedule against actual progress. This helps in identifying delays and deviations early, allowing for proactive management.
  • Critical Paths: Identify tasks that are critical to project completion. Managing these tasks effectively ensures that your project stays on track.
  • Milestones: Mark key project milestones to track significant achievements and keep your team focused on critical deadlines.
  • Sync with monday.com: The integration ensures that all project data remains synchronized across platforms, maintaining consistency and accuracy in your project management.

Gantt Chart Integration1020×574 61.4 KB

3. Workload Management:

  • Resource Allocation: Assign tasks based on team availability to ensure balanced workloads and optimal productivity. This prevents overloading team members and ensures that resources are used efficiently.
  • Capacity Planning: Monitor and manage team capacity to avoid burnout and ensure that all projects progress smoothly. This helps in maintaining a sustainable work pace and meeting deadlines.

Workload Management1020×574 65.1 KB

=>>Learn how to get started with PPM in monday.com using this guide

Benefits of Using Advanced Gantt Chart Integration for PPM:

  1. Improved Project Visibility: The Gantt chart’s visual representation of project timelines and dependencies makes it easier to understand the overall project landscape and anticipate potential challenges.
  2. Efficient Scheduling and Resource Management: Auto-scheduling and resource allocation features streamline project planning, ensuring that tasks are assigned and completed in an efficient and timely manner.
  3. Better Control and Alignment: With features like baselines, critical paths, and milestones, you can better control project progress and ensure alignment with strategic objectives.

By leveraging these advanced features, you can transform monday.com into a powerful tool for managing complex project portfolios, ensuring that you stay on top of project timelines, resource allocation, and overall project health. This solution not only bridges the gaps in monday.com’s native capabilities but also empowers you to optimize your project management processes effortlessly.

For more details and to get started, learn at this: PPM for monday.com

These resources will help you explore the full potential of Project Portfolio Management in monday.com and guide you through setting up and using the advanced features effectively.

posts.author: Miccyox (Miccyox)
posts.cookedHtml: I found that breaking down the projects into smaller, manageable chunks really helps. In my experience, using the dashboard to visualize progress across all projects at once made a big difference. Also, syncing tasks and deadlines with your calendar keeps things flowing smoothly. After going through a PMP Certification, I learned a lot about balancing multiple projects at once, and it’s been a huge help with staying organized and prioritizing effectively. monday.com has all the tools to support that, but the key is setting things up in a way that keeps everyone on track and aligned.

posts.author: Riceal-ey (Riceal)
posts.cookedHtml: my issue with PPM is that its missing a vital P . How did you skip the Program between the Portfolio and Project layers? This is more of a Project Program Management, and it would be great to have the Portfolio Management layer above it.
